name: White Rose
description_short: White Rose is not a consumer finance company. It is a division of Jackson & Perkins selling white rose bush plants and gardening supplies online.
description_long: White Rose is a product line operated by Jackson & Perkins, a horticulture and gardening retailer. The company sells white rose bushes and related gardening products through their website at jacksonandperkins.com. White Rose itself is not a financial services provider—it is a catalog of ornamental plants, specifically white-flowering rose varieties in multiple forms including hybrid teas, floribundas, grandifloras, climbers, and ground covers. Jackson & Perkins ships gardening products within Canada, the United States, and U.S. territories. The website describes white roses in decorative and horticultural terms, emphasizing their aesthetic qualities, fragrance profiles, bloom sizes, and compatibility with different USDA hardiness zones. Products listed include individual rose bushes priced between $39.95 and $59.95, available in various formats such as 2-quart containers and bareroot trees. The retailer also sells supplementary gardening products like fertilizer and transplant stress relief. This is fundamentally a plant retailer with e-commerce functionality, cookie management for website optimization, and standard retail features like shopping carts, order tracking, and seasonal shipping schedules. There are no financial services, lending products, credit-related offerings, or payday alternatives present on the White Rose product pages.
services: Sale of white rose bush plants (hybrid tea, floribunda, grandiflora, noisette, romantica varieties)
Fragrance profile filtering (slight, moderate, strong intensity)
Fragrance type categorization (fruity, spicy, sweet, floral)
Bloom size and petal count specifications for plant selection
Bareroot and container plant formats (24-inch bareroot trees, 36-inch bareroot trees, 2-quart containers)
Rose fertilizer products (Jackson & Perkins Select Rose Fertilizer)
Transplant stress relief products (Organic Diehard brand)
Shipping to Canada, United States, and U.S. territories
Customer ratings and reviews on product pages
pros: Offers white rose varieties across multiple hardiness zones (3-10) for broad geographic availability
Detailed filterable search by fragrance type (fruity, spicy, sweet, floral) and intensity level
Multiple rose formats available including hybrid teas, floribundas, climbers, and ground covers
Free printed catalog available for customers wanting reference materials
Products show verified customer ratings (multiple 5-star reviews displayed)
Supplementary products available (fertilizer, transplant stress relief) for complete garden care
Ships to Canada, United States, and U.S. territories with published shipping schedule
cons: Product prices ($39.95–$59.95 per plant) represent significant gardening investment with no financing options
Plant-based products carry inherent risk of shipping damage or poor establishment despite care instructions
Limited product quantity shown on reviewed pages (approximately 6 white rose varieties visible), suggesting incomplete inventory display
No customer service contact information, phone number, or live chat visible on product pages
Website cookie preferences and privacy policy are standard e-commerce boilerplate with no specialized protections
best_for: Home gardeners seeking white rose varieties for aesthetic landscape design
Gardeners in specific USDA zones looking for cold or heat-hardy rose cultivars
Customers preferring fragrant roses for sensory garden experiences
diagnosis: White Rose is not a consumer finance product and should not be listed on CreditDoc. It is a horticulture retailer selling ornamental rose plants. This profile exists due to miscategorization in the underlying database and has no relevance to credit, lending, financial alternatives, or consumer finance research.
typical_results_timeline: Membership approval and account opening typically takes 1-3 business days. Loan decisions are usually faster than traditional banks.
